data.title: Plugin release channels: stable + dev marketplace entries with maintainer release script
data.description:
## Context

Today, every commit merged to main propagates to every downstream Claude Code project that has installed the DSO plugin via /plugin marketplace add navapbc/digital-service-orchestra. Plugin maintainers at Nava cannot iterate on the plugin without exposing in-flight changes to consumers, and consumers have no way to opt into either a stable channel or a continuously-updated dev channel. This blocks routine improvements (tightening hooks, adjusting agent prompts, evolving skill behavior) because every change instantly becomes everyone's change.

This epic introduces the Anthropic-recommended two-channel pattern: a single marketplace.json on main exposes two plugin entries — dso (stable, pinned via git-subdir to the latest release tag) and dso-dev (pinned to main). Maintainers cut releases via a new scripts/release.sh at the repo root, gated on CI green, a clean working tree, and full local validation. Existing clients silently land on the stable channel on their next marketplace refresh.

## Success Criteria

- After the cutover commit, a downstream client running /plugin marketplace update sees their installed dso plugin re-resolved to the latest release tag (not to main HEAD).
- Clients can run /plugin install dso-dev@<marketplace-name> to subscribe to per-merge updates from main. Validation signal: a /plugin marketplace update after a merge to main re-resolves dso-dev to the new commit.
- A merge to main does not change the content that dso consumers see on their next marketplace refresh — only dso-dev consumers move forward.
- scripts/release.sh cuts a release only after confirming (a) CI is green on the commit being tagged, (b) the working tree is clean and the local branch is main up-to-date with origin/main, and (c) plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh --ci passes; the script aborts with a clear error message naming the failed precondition otherwise.
- The first stable release tag (v1.13.0, the next minor bump from the current 1.12.32) is cut from main during epic execution, and the dso entry in marketplace.json is pinned to it via git-subdir.
- INSTALL.md documents both channels (install commands for dso and dso-dev, recommendation to enable auto-update on the marketplace via the /plugin UI, and a one-line statement of the version semantics — dso advances on tagged releases, dso-dev advances on every merge to main).

## Dependencies

None. The version auto-bump in merge-to-main.sh is a precondition and already exists.

## Approach

Staged three-PR rollout (atomic, individually testable, no negative client impact along the way):

- PR 1 — Restructure marketplace.json: convert the existing single relative-path entry into two git-subdir entries (dso and dso-dev), both pointing at main initially. No behavior change for existing clients; opens the dev channel for early adopters.
- PR 2 — Add scripts/release.sh (bash, ~80-150 lines) at the repo root; add INSTALL.md documentation for both channels. Still no client behavior change; the script is testable but unused.
- PR 3 (cutover) — Run scripts/release.sh to cut v1.13.0; commit the resulting marketplace.json edit that re-pins the dso entry's source.ref from main to v1.13.0. This is the moment existing clients silently shift to the stable channel on their next refresh.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: external integration (Claude Code plugin marketplace mechanism), novel pattern (release-channel design), user-requested up-front research
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): not triggered — gracefully degraded with rationale (architecture fully constrained by Anthropic docs and user-driven decisions; minimal adversarial surface). Available on request via approval gate option (b).
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
- Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0): not triggered
- Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5): not triggered
- LLM-instruction signal (Step 5): not triggered
- Scale context (Step 0): not applicable (no volume decision)
